# MACRO REPORT — 2026-05-01
**Delivered:** 11:43 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Markets enter May with a powerful tailwind from April's best monthly performance since 2020, as the S&P 500 posted a +9.5% gain for the month and the Nasdaq surged nearly +14%, driven by blowout Magnificent Seven AI earnings. However, the dominant macro narrative remains a precarious balancing act: robust corporate earnings and resilient labour markets (jobless claims at 55-year lows) are colliding with an energy-driven inflation surge tied to the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict and the indefinite closure of the Strait of Hormuz [Source: https://nypost.com/2026/04/30/business/us-economy-grows-2-as-layoffs-plunge-to-55-year-low-inflation-lingers/]. The **single most important variable for today's session** is the trajectory of oil prices and any diplomatic signals on U.S.-Iran negotiations, with Brent having already pulled back sharply from the $125+ peaks seen earlier this week. The FOMC held rates steady for a third consecutive meeting while signalling that the easing bias is under internal threat, with three dissenters questioning its inclusion — a hawkish tilt that reinforces the "higher-for-longer" narrative [Source: https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/monetary20260429a.htm].

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

The macro backdrop as of May 1, 2026 is defined by a genuine tension between exceptional corporate earnings momentum and structurally elevated inflation. The U.S. economy expanded at a 2% annualised pace in Q1 2026, a resilient figure given the energy shock, while initial jobless claims plunged to 189,000 — the lowest since 1969 — underscoring labour market durability [Source: https://nypost.com/2026/04/30/business/us-economy-grows-2-as-layoffs-plunge-to-55-year-low-inflation-lingers/]. The S&P 500 closed April at 7,209 (+1.02% on the day), cementing its best monthly gain since November 2020, while the Dow surged +1.62% to 49,652 as breadth broadened out [Source: https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-today-dow-jumps-750-points-sp-500-nasdaq-notch-record-highs-to-cap-best-month-for-stocks-since-2020-233115747.html].

The inflation picture has materially worsened. Core PCE rose to 3.2% year-over-year in March (up from 3.0% in February), while headline PCE printed at 3.5%, both driven by the Iran war's energy shock [Source: https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/key-inflation-meter-jumped-again-125007996.html]. CPI stands at 3.32% YoY as of March data [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL], and PPI momentum remains elevated at +1.78% MoM [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/PPIACO]. The Fed's preferred core PCE running above target has effectively killed near-term rate cut expectations, with Morgan Stanley's Ellen Zentner noting the "stubborn" reading will likely persuade the Fed against future rate cuts [Source: https://malaysia.news.yahoo.com/key-inflation-meter-jumped-again-125007996.html].

The Federal Reserve held rates steady at 3.50–3.75% at its April meeting — the final meeting under Chair Jerome Powell's tenure — but the hawkish internal dissent is significant. Three FOMC members opposed the "easing bias" language, and one (Miran) preferred an immediate 25bp cut, making for a fractured committee [Source: https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/monetary20260429a.htm]. The effective fed funds rate sits at 3.64% [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS], with the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.42% and the 2-year at 3.92%, maintaining a modestly inverted curve [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10]. Globally, major central banks — the ECB, BoJ and BoE — are all signalling potential rate hikes to combat energy-driven inflation spillovers, a hawkish pivot from earlier easing cycles [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-30/big-central-banks-say-rate-hikes-are-getting-closer/].

Earnings season has been a key pillar of equity resilience. Microsoft reported AI business revenue at a $37 billion annual run rate (up 123% YoY), Meta, Alphabet, and Amazon all raised capex projections, and the S&P 500 is on track for its sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit earnings growth [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/earnings-live-updates-sofi-stock-tumbles-alphabet-stock-jumps-as-magnificent-7-results-roll-in-111414129.html]. Today's session brings Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), Moderna, Colgate-Palmolive, and Dominion Energy results, alongside the ISM Manufacturing PMI — a key gauge of how the energy shock is filtering into the real economy [Source: https://www.tipranks.com/news/why-are-stock-market-futures-up-today-4-30-26].

The World Bank has warned that the energy shock impact from the U.S.-Iran conflict will be felt globally, with the UK economy expected to be the hardest-hit advanced economy per the IMF [Source: https://www.axios.com/2026/05/01/uk-economy-iran-labour]. Goldman Sachs estimates Strait of Hormuz exports have fallen to just 4% of normal levels, with stalled nuclear negotiations keeping supply tight [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/30/oil-prices-today-brent-wti-us-iran-war-trump.html]. VIX sits at 17.01, elevated but not at crisis levels, reflecting ongoing but partially priced geopolitical risk [Source: Yahoo Finance (^VIX)].

---

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

- The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran has caused the indefinite closure of the Strait of Hormuz, with Goldman Sachs estimating exports through the chokepoint have fallen to just 4% of normal levels, keeping global oil supply severely constrained. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/30/oil-prices-today-brent-wti-us-iran-war-trump.html]
- Brent crude surged past $125 a barrel earlier this week as stalled U.S.-Iran nuclear negotiations raised doubts over the permanent reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. [Source: https://apnews.com/article/oil-trump-iran-stocks-markets-42120b305ce6298712931e79b66a20de]
- The Wall Street Journal reported, citing U.S. officials, that President Trump instructed aides to prepare for an extended blockade of Iranian exports, further diminishing near-term de-escalation hopes. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/30/oil-prices-today-brent-wti-us-iran-war-trump.html]
- The Iran war is cited as the primary driver of energy-cost inflation in the U.S., with the FOMC statement explicitly referencing the conflict as contributing to "a high level of uncertainty about the economic outlook." [Source: https://malaysia.news.yahoo.com/key-inflation-meter-jumped-again-125007996.html]
- The U.S. Fed's outlook for further rate cuts in 2026 has dimmed significantly following the conflict, with investors now pricing in no Fed cuts this year. [Source: https://www.realtor.com/news/trends/fed-interest-rate-decision-kevin-warsh-jerome-powell-april-2026/]
- International LNG prices have surged, with TTF rising 35% and the Japan-Korea Marker (JKM) up 51% since the Strait of Hormuz closure, while U.S. Henry Hub prices have declined due to limited export capacity. [Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/04/29/international-lng-prices-rise-amid-strait-of-hormuz-closure/]
- The World Bank warned the energy shock from the conflict will be felt globally, with the UK economy projected to be the worst-hit advanced economy. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/30/world-bank-energy-shock-impact-will-be-felt-globally.html]
- Israel's economy is expected to outperform many major developed markets in 2026, with the IMF forecasting 4.4% growth in 2027, though labour shortages and weaker consumer spending are emerging headwinds. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/30/israel-economy-stock-market-tel-aviv-trump-netanyahu-gdp-us-inflation.html]

### Energy

**Brent Crude — ~$110.96** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
- Brent fell sharply -2.68% today to $110.96, pulling back from the $125+ levels seen earlier in the week, with futures contract roll-over and some reported hopes of de-escalation in U.S.-Iran tensions contributing to the pullback. However, Goldman Sachs notes Hormuz flows remain at just 4% of normal, keeping the structural supply squeeze intact. [Source: Yahoo Finance (BZ=F); https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/30/oil-prices-today-brent-wti-us-iran-war-trump.html]
- Key levels: $105.00 support | $118.00 resistance
- Catalyst: U.S.-Iran diplomatic developments and any Strait of Hormuz reopening signals

**WTI Crude — ~$104.22** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
- WTI pulled back -0.81% to $104.22, a more modest decline than Brent, with the Brent-WTI spread widening reflecting the disproportionate impact of Hormuz disruption on internationally-traded crude grades. [Source: Yahoo Finance (CL=F)]
- Key levels: $98.00 support | $112.00 resistance
- Catalyst: EIA inventory data and Iran negotiation headlines

**TTF Natural Gas — ~N/A (Henry Hub used as proxy)** | Bias: Bullish
- TTF prices surged 35% since the Strait of Hormuz closure as European LNG import competition intensified; the Henry Hub price at $2.787 reflects the divergence — the U.S. domestic market is insulated by a gas glut even as international LNG prices soar. [Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/04/29/international-lng-prices-rise-amid-strait-of-hormuz-closure/]
- Key levels: $2.50 support | $3.20 resistance (Henry Hub)
- Catalyst: U.S. LNG export authorisation updates and Hormuz closure duration

**Henry Hub Natural Gas — ~$2.787** | Bias: Neutral
- Henry Hub edged up +0.72% today but remains structurally depressed — down ~9% since February — as U.S. domestic oversupply prevents the global energy shock from fully transmitting into U.S. natural gas prices, creating a unique insulation effect. [Source: Yahoo Finance (NG=F); https://www.axios.com/2026/04/30/natural-gas-texas-negative]
- Key levels: $2.50 support | $3.20 resistance
- Catalyst: LNG export terminal capacity decisions and storage data

- The S&P 500 closed April at record highs, up +1.02% on April 30th to 7,209, capping the best monthly performance since November 2020 (+9.5% for the month). [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GSPC); https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/earnings-live-updates-sofi-stock-tumbles-alphabet-stock-jumps-as-magnificent-7-results-roll-in-111414129.html]

### Key Upcoming Catalysts

1. **Friday, May 1 (TODAY):** ISM Manufacturing PMI (U.S.) — key gauge of how energy shock is filtering into industrial activity; also Exxon (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) earnings, highly relevant given the oil price environment. [Source: https://www.tipranks.com/news/why-are-stock-market-futures-up-today-4-30-26]

2. **Week of May 4-8:** Jerome Powell's tenure ends as Fed Chair — Kevin Warsh widely expected as successor. New Chair's first communications will be market-moving, particularly regarding the easing bias language opposed by three FOMC members. [Source: https://www.realtor.com/news/trends/fed-interest-rate-decision-kevin-warsh-jerome-powell-april-2026/]

3. **Week of May 4-8:** ECB policy meeting — central bank flagged potential rate hikes "as soon as June" to combat energy-driven inflation; any formal guidance shift would be highly market-moving for EUR and European equities. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-30/big-central-banks-say-rate-hikes-are-getting-closer/]

4. **Week of May 4-8:** BoJ policy follow-up communications — after "unusually blunt" rate hike signals, markets are on alert for confirmation of a near-term hike that would accelerate JPY appreciation and pressure USD/JPY below 158. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-30/big-central-banks-say-rate-hikes-are-getting-closer/]

5. **Ongoing:** U.S.-Iran nuclear negotiations and Strait of Hormuz status — this binary geopolitical variable remains the single largest swing factor for oil prices, inflation expectations, and global risk sentiment. Any credible de-escalation signal would trigger a sharp oil selloff and equity/crypto rally; further escalation risks a stagflationary shock. [Source: https://apnews.com/article/oil-trump-iran-stocks-markets-42120b305ce6298712931e79b66a20de]
